description: 2.0 seeds: README pointers for the four new primitive-kind directories (skills, agents, commands, rules). Pure additions — add_file is idempotent and never overwrites. Re-run `keystone init` to backfill the full canonical templates (including the keystone:* maintenance skills).

operations.content:
# Skills

Project-authored Claude Code skills. Each skill lives at
`skills/<id>/SKILL.md` and declares canonical primitive frontmatter
(`kind: skill`, `id`, `description`, `triggers`).

This is the source of truth. `keystone project` regenerates the host
projection at `.claude/skills/<id>/SKILL.md`.

See `docs/ports/primitive.md` for the descriptor shape.

# Subagents

Project-authored subagent definitions. Each subagent lives at
`agents/<id>.md` and declares canonical primitive frontmatter
(`kind: subagent`, `id`, `description`, `tools`).

This is the source of truth. `keystone project` regenerates the host
projection at `.claude/agents/<id>.md`.

See `docs/ports/primitive.md` for the descriptor shape.

# Slash commands

Project-authored slash commands. Each command lives at
`commands/<id>.md` and declares canonical primitive frontmatter
(`kind: command`, `id`, `description`, `args`).

This is the source of truth. `keystone project` regenerates the host
projection at `.claude/commands/<id>.md`.

See `docs/ports/primitive.md` for the descriptor shape.

# Rules (agent abstraction)

Host-native flavor of a directive (Cursor `.mdc`, plain `CLAUDE.md`
block). Each rule lives at `rules/<id>.md` and declares canonical
primitive frontmatter (`kind: rule`, `id`, `description`).

Framework default: use `guides/` instead. Reach for `rules/` only when
the directive matches a host-native rule slot exactly (e.g. a Cursor
rule that should project 1:1 to `.cursor/rules/`).

See `docs/ports/primitive.md` for the descriptor shape.
